title: atriplex patula subsp. obtusa
intro: Atriplex patula subsp. obtusa, also known as orach, is a subspecies of the Atriplex patula plant, a type of saltbush native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its high content of mucilages. The health benefits of Atriplex patula subsp. obtusa include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of orach often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and stems, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
